Name: RML Customer ID
Description: [customer_id] Customer ID uniquely identifies a retail customer across the Bronze, Silver and Gold layers. Primary key in raw_customers.csv, foreign key in raw_orders.csv and gold_customer_360. Governed constraint (numeric range): 4-digit integer, range 1001-1050 in the demo seed.
Classifications: Retail Medallion Lakehouse >> Data Governance >> Business Data
Related Terms: Retail Medallion Lakehouse >> Customer >> Customer ID
Definition: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?><DataClasses xmlns="http://www.ibm.com/infosphere/ia/classification/DataclassesDefinition"><DataClass id="RML_CustomerID" name="RML Customer ID" description="Matches source column customer_id" priority="100" active="true" provider="CUSTOM"><RegularExpressionClassifier scope="value"><RegularExpression>^[0-9]{4}$</RegularExpression></RegularExpressionClassifier><ColumnNameFilter><ColumnNameRegularExpression>^customer_id$</ColumnNameRegularExpression></ColumnNameFilter></DataClass></DataClasses>

Classifications: Retail Medallion Lakehouse >> Data Governance >> Personal Data

Name: RML Customer Email
Description: [email] Customer Email is the primary contact channel and the only direct personal identifier in the demo seed. Subject to masking and data-protection rules (PII/GDPR). Governed constraint (format): standard RFC 5321 email address.
Classifications: Retail Medallion Lakehouse >> Data Governance >> Business Data
Related Terms: Retail Medallion Lakehouse >> Customer >> Customer Email
Definition: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?><DataClasses xmlns="http://www.ibm.com/infosphere/ia/classification/DataclassesDefinition"><DataClass id="RML_CustomerEmail" name="RML Customer Email" description="Matches source column email" priority="100" active="true" provider="CUSTOM"><RegularExpressionClassifier scope="value"><RegularExpression>^[A-Za-z0-9._%+\-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.\-]+\.[A-Za-z]{2,}$</RegularExpression></RegularExpressionClassifier><ColumnNameFilter><ColumnNameRegularExpression>^email$</ColumnNameRegularExpression></ColumnNameFilter></DataClass></DataClasses>

Classifications: Retail Medallion Lakehouse >> Data Governance >> Personal Data

Name: RML Customer Country
Description: [country] Customer Country holds an ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code. Governed allowed values from the seed: US, GB, DE, FR, IT, ES, NL. Should conform to the Country reference data set for consistent regional segmentation.
Classifications: Retail Medallion Lakehouse >> Data Governance >> Business Data
Related Terms: Retail Medallion Lakehouse >> Customer >> Customer Country
Definition: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?><DataClasses xmlns="http://www.ibm.com/infosphere/ia/classification/DataclassesDefinition"><DataClass id="RML_CustomerCountry" name="RML Customer Country" description="Matches source column country" priority="100" active="true" provider="CUSTOM"><RegularExpressionClassifier scope="value"><RegularExpression>^(US|GB|DE|FR|IT|ES|NL)$</RegularExpression></RegularExpressionClassifier><ColumnNameFilter><ColumnNameRegularExpression>^country$</ColumnNameRegularExpression></ColumnNameFilter></DataClass></DataClasses>

Name: RML Customer 360
Description: [lifetime_value] Customer 360 targets the lifetime_value column in gold_customer_360 — the primary monetary KPI of the 360 view. Profiles non-negative decimal amounts (up to 2 d.p.) representing cumulative net spend across completed orders.
Classifications: Retail Medallion Lakehouse >> Data Governance >> Business Data
Definition: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?><DataClasses xmlns="http://www.ibm.com/infosphere/ia/classification/DataclassesDefinition"><DataClass id="RML_Customer360" name="RML Customer 360" description="Matches source column lifetime_value" priority="100" active="true" provider="CUSTOM"><RegularExpressionClassifier scope="value"><RegularExpression>^(?:0|[1-9][0-9]{0,9})(?:\.[0-9]{1,2})?$</RegularExpression></RegularExpressionClassifier><ColumnNameFilter><ColumnNameRegularExpression>^lifetime_value$</ColumnNameRegularExpression></ColumnNameFilter></DataClass></DataClasses>

Name: RML Product ID
Description: [product_id] Product ID uniquely identifies a product in raw_products.csv. Foreign key in raw_order_items.csv. Governed constraint (numeric range): 4-digit integer starting with 2, range 2001-2020 in the demo seed.
Classifications: Retail Medallion Lakehouse >> Data Governance >> Business Data
Related Terms: Retail Medallion Lakehouse >> Product >> Product ID
Definition: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?><DataClasses xmlns="http://www.ibm.com/infosphere/ia/classification/DataclassesDefinition"><DataClass id="RML_ProductID" name="RML Product ID" description="Matches source column product_id" priority="100" active="true" provider="CUSTOM"><RegularExpressionClassifier scope="value"><RegularExpression>^2[0-9]{3}$</RegularExpression></RegularExpressionClassifier><ColumnNameFilter><ColumnNameRegularExpression>^product_id$</ColumnNameRegularExpression></ColumnNameFilter></DataClass></DataClasses>

Name: RML Product Category
Description: [category] Product Category classifies a product into one of five controlled domains: Electronics, Grocery, Home, Office, Outdoor. Used as the grain of gold_category_performance. Must conform to the Product Category reference data set.
Classifications: Retail Medallion Lakehouse >> Data Governance >> Business Data
Related Terms: Retail Medallion Lakehouse >> Product >> Product Category
Definition: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?><DataClasses xmlns="http://www.ibm.com/infosphere/ia/classification/DataclassesDefinition"><DataClass id="RML_ProductCategory" name="RML Product Category" description="Matches source column category" priority="100" active="true" provider="CUSTOM"><RegularExpressionClassifier scope="value"><RegularExpression>^(Electronics|Grocery|Home|Office|Outdoor)$</RegularExpression></RegularExpressionClassifier><ColumnNameFilter><ColumnNameRegularExpression>^(category|product_category)$</ColumnNameRegularExpression></ColumnNameFilter></DataClass></DataClasses>

Name: RML Unit Price
Description: [unit_price] Unit Price is the list price for one unit of a product before discount. Combined with order quantity and discount_pct to compute the Silver net_amount column. Governed constraint (monetary): non-negative amount up to 5 integer and 2 decimal digits.
Classifications: Retail Medallion Lakehouse >> Data Governance >> Business Data
Related Terms: Retail Medallion Lakehouse >> Product >> Unit Price
Definition: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?><DataClasses xmlns="http://www.ibm.com/infosphere/ia/classification/DataclassesDefinition"><DataClass id="RML_UnitPrice" name="RML Unit Price" description="Matches source column unit_price" priority="100" active="true" provider="CUSTOM"><RegularExpressionClassifier scope="value"><RegularExpression>^(?:0|[1-9][0-9]{0,4})(?:\.[0-9]{1,2})?$</RegularExpression></RegularExpressionClassifier><ColumnNameFilter><ColumnNameRegularExpression>^unit_price$</ColumnNameRegularExpression></ColumnNameFilter></DataClass></DataClasses>

Name: RML Order ID
Description: [order_id] Order ID uniquely identifies a purchase event in raw_orders.csv. Foreign key in raw_order_items.csv. Governed constraint (numeric range): 4-digit integer starting with 3, range 3001-3500 in the demo seed.
Classifications: Retail Medallion Lakehouse >> Data Governance >> Business Data
Related Terms: Retail Medallion Lakehouse >> Order >> Order ID
Definition: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?><DataClasses xmlns="http://www.ibm.com/infosphere/ia/classification/DataclassesDefinition"><DataClass id="RML_OrderID" name="RML Order ID" description="Matches source column order_id" priority="100" active="true" provider="CUSTOM"><RegularExpressionClassifier scope="value"><RegularExpression>^3[0-9]{3}$</RegularExpression></RegularExpressionClassifier><ColumnNameFilter><ColumnNameRegularExpression>^order_id$</ColumnNameRegularExpression></ColumnNameFilter></DataClass></DataClasses>

Name: RML Order Status
Description: [status] Order Status is the lifecycle state code of an order. Only completed orders count toward net_revenue and lifetime_value. Governed constraint (allowed values): completed, returned, pending, cancelled.
Classifications: Retail Medallion Lakehouse >> Data Governance >> Business Data
Related Terms: Retail Medallion Lakehouse >> Order >> Order Status
Definition: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?><DataClasses xmlns="http://www.ibm.com/infosphere/ia/classification/DataclassesDefinition"><DataClass id="RML_OrderStatus" name="RML Order Status" description="Matches source column status" priority="100" active="true" provider="CUSTOM"><RegularExpressionClassifier scope="value"><RegularExpression>^(completed|returned|pending|cancelled)$</RegularExpression></RegularExpressionClassifier><ColumnNameFilter><ColumnNameRegularExpression>^(status|order_status)$</ColumnNameRegularExpression></ColumnNameFilter></DataClass></DataClasses>

Name: RML Order Quantity
Description: [quantity] Order Quantity is the number of product units on a single order line in raw_order_items.csv. Summed into units_sold in gold_daily_sales. Governed constraint (numeric range): positive integer from 1 through 99.
Classifications: Retail Medallion Lakehouse >> Data Governance >> Business Data
Related Terms: Retail Medallion Lakehouse >> Order >> Order Quantity
Definition: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?><DataClasses xmlns="http://www.ibm.com/infosphere/ia/classification/DataclassesDefinition"><DataClass id="RML_OrderQuantity" name="RML Order Quantity" description="Matches source column quantity" priority="100" active="true" provider="CUSTOM"><RegularExpressionClassifier scope="value"><RegularExpression>^[1-9][0-9]?$</RegularExpression></RegularExpressionClassifier><ColumnNameFilter><ColumnNameRegularExpression>^quantity$</ColumnNameRegularExpression></ColumnNameFilter></DataClass></DataClasses>

Name: RML Net Amount
Description: [net_amount] Net Amount is the computed monetary value of an order line: unit_price * quantity * (1 - discount_pct). The atomic revenue measure in the Silver layer; rolls up into net_revenue and lifetime_value. Governed constraint (monetary): non-negative amount up to 10 integer and 2 decimal digits.
Classifications: Retail Medallion Lakehouse >> Data Governance >> Business Data
Related Terms: Retail Medallion Lakehouse >> Order >> Net Amount
Definition: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?><DataClasses xmlns="http://www.ibm.com/infosphere/ia/classification/DataclassesDefinition"><DataClass id="RML_NetAmount" name="RML Net Amount" description="Matches source column net_amount" priority="100" active="true" provider="CUSTOM"><RegularExpressionClassifier scope="value"><RegularExpression>^(?:0|[1-9][0-9]{0,9})(?:\.[0-9]{1,2})?$</RegularExpression></RegularExpressionClassifier><ColumnNameFilter><ColumnNameRegularExpression>^net_amount$</ColumnNameRegularExpression></ColumnNameFilter></DataClass></DataClasses>

Name: RML Daily Sales
Description: [order_date] Daily Sales targets the order_date grain column in gold_daily_sales, a PARQUET table partitioned by month(order_date). Profiles ISO 8601 calendar dates (YYYY-MM-DD) derived from the order_ts timestamp in the Silver layer.
Classifications: Retail Medallion Lakehouse >> Data Governance >> Business Data
Related Terms: Retail Medallion Lakehouse >> Gold Sales >> Daily Sales
Definition: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?><DataClasses xmlns="http://www.ibm.com/infosphere/ia/classification/DataclassesDefinition"><DataClass id="RML_DailySales" name="RML Daily Sales" description="Matches source column order_date" priority="100" active="true" provider="CUSTOM"><RegularExpressionClassifier scope="value"><RegularExpression>^\d{4}-(0[1-9]|1[0-2])-(0[1-9]|[12]\d|3[01])$</RegularExpression></RegularExpressionClassifier><ColumnNameFilter><ColumnNameRegularExpression>^order_date$</ColumnNameRegularExpression></ColumnNameFilter></DataClass></DataClasses>

Name: RML Net Revenue
Description: [net_revenue] Net Revenue is the primary Gold KPI: sum of net_amount for completed orders only. Present as net_revenue in gold_daily_sales and as total_revenue in gold_category_performance. Governed constraint (monetary): non-negative aggregate up to 10 integer and 2 decimal digits.
Classifications: Retail Medallion Lakehouse >> Data Governance >> Business Data
Related Terms: Retail Medallion Lakehouse >> Gold Sales >> Net Revenue
Definition: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?><DataClasses xmlns="http://www.ibm.com/infosphere/ia/classification/DataclassesDefinition"><DataClass id="RML_NetRevenue" name="RML Net Revenue" description="Matches source column net_revenue" priority="100" active="true" provider="CUSTOM"><RegularExpressionClassifier scope="value"><RegularExpression>^(?:0|[1-9][0-9]{0,9})(?:\.[0-9]{1,2})?$</RegularExpression></RegularExpressionClassifier><ColumnNameFilter><ColumnNameRegularExpression>^(net_revenue|total_revenue)$</ColumnNameRegularExpression></ColumnNameFilter></DataClass></DataClasses>

Name: RML Category Performance
Description: [total_orders] Category Performance targets the total_orders dimension in gold_category_performance — a count of distinct completed orders per product category. Governed constraint (numeric range): non-negative integer up to 9,999,999.
Classifications: Retail Medallion Lakehouse >> Data Governance >> Business Data
Related Terms: Retail Medallion Lakehouse >> Gold Sales >> Category Performance
Definition: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?><DataClasses xmlns="http://www.ibm.com/infosphere/ia/classification/DataclassesDefinition"><DataClass id="RML_CategoryPerformance" name="RML Category Performance" description="Matches source column total_orders" priority="100" active="true" provider="CUSTOM"><RegularExpressionClassifier scope="value"><RegularExpression>^(?:0|[1-9][0-9]{0,6})$</RegularExpression></RegularExpressionClassifier><ColumnNameFilter><ColumnNameRegularExpression>^(total_orders|order_count)$</ColumnNameRegularExpression></ColumnNameFilter></DataClass></DataClasses>
